q: The men always (collect) the rubbish in the morning.
a: The men always collect the rubbish in the morning.

q: She (brush) her teeth every day.
a: She brushes her teeth every day.

q: Ducks (have) webbed feet and they (swim).
a: Ducks have webbed feet and they swim.

q: Anne (plan) to set up her own business if she (have) the money.
a: Anne plans to set up her own business if she has the money.

q: He (not drink) tea always.
a: He doesn't drink tea always.

q: What (make) the sun shine?
a: What makes the sun shine?

q: We often (go) for a swim in the lake. Sometimes we (camp) near it during weekends.
a: We often go for a swim in the lake. Sometimes we camp near it during weekends.

q: She often (miss) her country of origion.
a: She often misses her country of origion.

q: I (write) to my pen-pal once a week.
a: I write to my pen-pal once a week.

q: My family (come) from England.
a: My family comes from England.

q: He never (go) out on weekdays.
a: He never goes out on weekdays.

q: I usually (read) a book when I (be) free.
a: I usually read a book when I am free.

q: My friend (speak) Chinese but he (not know) how to write it.
a: My friend speaks Chinese but he does not know how to write it.

q: If you (fail) this test, you have to repeat the subject.
a: If you fail this test, you have to repeat the subject.

q: The play (begin) at two and (end) at four p.m.
a: The play begins at two and ends at four p.m.

q: Aung Htet (work) at the bank.
a: Aung Htet works at the bank.

q: My mother (buy) fresh bread every morning.
a: My mother buys fresh bread every morning.

q: If I don't (do) well in my exams this year, my mother won't (allow) me to visit friends in Europe.
a: If I don't do well in my exams this year, my mother won't allow me to visit friends in Europe.

q: She always (have) cereal for breakfast.
a: She always has cereal for breakfast.

q: The earth (move) around the sun.
a: The earth moves around the sun.

q: I (mix) in some salt and (bring) the soup to a boil.
a: I mix in some salt and bring the soup to a boil.

q: My grandfather never (smoke) cigarettes; he always (smoke) a pipe.
a: My grandfather never smokes cigarettes; he always smokes a pipe.

q: We (make) our beds every morning.
a: We make our beds every morning.

q: Do you (feel) guilty when your child (watch) TV?
a: Do you feel guilty when your child watches TV?

q: He (be) good at football. He (practise) every evening.
a: He is good at football. He practises every evening.

q: The bus (leave) at six p.m.
a: The bus leaves at six p.m.

q: I (wash) my face every morning.
a: I wash my face every morning.

q: The teacher (tell) us a story every Friday.
a: The teacher tells us a story every Friday.

q: Cocks (crow); a hen (cackle).
a: Cocks crow; a hen cackles.

q: My mother (wash) her hair every Saturday.
a: My mother washes her hair every Saturday.

q: Mosquitoes (be) harmful insects. They (suck) our blood and (spread) diseases.
a: Mosquitoes are harmful insects. They suck our blood and spread diseases.

q: Win Win (be) always nervous when she (go) to the dentist.
a: Win Win is always nervous when she goes to the dentist.

q: I seldom (talk) to David. I (not like) him.
a: I seldom talk to David. I do not like him.

q: They always (get) up early and (practise) the piano.
a: They always get up early and practise the piano.

q: If you (quit) now, all your efforts will be wasted.
a: If you quit now, all your efforts will be wasted.
